requirements.title: Requirements source of truth exists and is complete.
Verifier script exists and executes static, runtime, performance checks.
Requirements gate test fails when verifier fails.
Verifier calls UI walkthrough and enforces clipping report pass.
Brand source is normalized to src/assets/brand/fixfox_logo_source.png.
Brand assets are generated from source (mark, @2x, ico).
App and window icons use FixFox brand icon.
No runtime dependency on legacy assets/brand paths.
Tracked-file inventory generated.
Architecture and runtime audit documented.
Rebuild verification report is generated by verifier.
Repo cleanup planning and notes are documented.
Long work is async/off UI thread via worker layer.
Task layer supports cancel/timeout signaling.
Click-to-feedback is immediate and UI stays responsive.
Time-to-first-paint is within defined threshold.
Centralized theme/token pipeline exists.
QSS styling applies to app and dialogs/popups.
Global combo down-arrow override is present.
No hardcoded absolute font paths; graceful fallback.
Single icon nav rail only (no legacy double nav).
Top app bar contains branded identity, search, quick check, overflow.
No duplicate Open Reports actions next to overflow.
About Qt removed.
No QSplitter usage in shell (static and runtime).
Help is integrated inside Settings flow.
Required icon assets exist (home/open_book/wrench/gear/reports/history/search/details/etc).
Required nav icon mapping: settings=gear fixes=wrench playbooks=open_book.
Icon loader supports caching and tinting.
Search control is expandable and animated in app bar.
Ctrl+K focuses search.
Search results stay open >=500ms while typing.
Search debounce and keyboard navigation exist.
Search index includes route and play registries.
Details sheet is hidden by default.
Details sheet has open toggle and open/close works.
Details content shows contextual summary and actions.
Onboarding flow is simple and consistent (Welcome -> Preferences -> First Action -> Finish).
Settings sidebar labels do not overlap/clipping at common sizes.
Settings includes reset/export controls.
Settings and dialogs update live with theme changes.
Home dashboard includes live CPU/RAM/Disk/session/task metrics.
Home includes last-updated and next best action guidance.
Playbooks page is a clean catalog with run/preview and metadata.
Play entries are uniquely categorized with no duplicates.
Diagnose page has structured findings and useful empty state CTA.
Fix execution is async with progress and cancel path.
Reports flow supports configure -> preview -> generate -> validate.
History page exposes search/filter/compare path.
Status area shows Idle/Running/Error state and task context.
UI applies accent + severity visual cues consistently.
Major pages avoid large dead zones with meaningful content.
ToolRunner and popup/dialog surfaces match theme style.
No clipping across walkthrough resolutions.
Toast notifications exist for task/export/copy/masking outcomes.
Global crash/error banner and copy-details path exist.
Task center/details keeps users informed about running jobs.
Shared empty-state component used across pages.
Repo cleanup plan contains keep/remove/archive proofs.
Repo cleanup notes document executed changes and proofs.
UI walkthrough screenshots + manifest generated.
Clipping report has no violations.
Smoke and unit tests pass.
Requirements gate test passes.
Play contract enforces preflight, outcome, evidence, rollback metadata.
Safe mode boundary (Basic safe-only, Pro advanced/admin with confirmations).
Capability matrix doc explains local/admin/org boundaries.
Guided fix wizard component exists and is reusable.
Unified evidence model exists and is used by runner summaries.
CI/test enforcement for dedupe/categorization/metadata is present.
Plain-language user summaries with technical output isolated.
RouteRegistry + PlayRegistry are single sources for routing/search alignment.
Admin-required plays show clear elevation story.
Risky play guardrails include backup/restore-point/reboot messaging.
Release checklist doc exists and includes required gates.
Dry-run harness exists for non-destructive validation.
Final run includes pushed commits and clean status.
